metadata.title: Regression: Analysis Wide Chart Table Overflow

slides.title: Maturity Score by Function
slides.strapline: Function-level averages highlight where executive attention should be concentrated over the next remediation cycle. This strapline is intentionally expanded to pressure body-region capacity after heading, chart, table, and source reservations are applied.
slides.heading: Function-level snapshot
body: Respond and Recover remain comparatively stronger, reflecting clearer incident and restoration process structure across assessed artifacts.
Detect and Protect remain the primary pressure points where operational execution and documented control design are not consistently aligned.
Focus near-term investment on detection engineering, monitoring governance, and policy-to-practice operating controls while sustaining gains in response and recovery disciplines.
Prioritize evidence traceability for objective ownership and cadence decisions where documentation currently relies on inferred practices.
Ensure remediation sequencing is tied to measurable control outcomes and board-level reporting expectations.
Maintain clear linkage between tactical action plans and category-level maturity movement assumptions.
Add an explicit decision log for deferred remediations so future reviews can validate risk acceptance rationale.

chart.source: Source: consolidatedReport.xlsx Summary tab, Maturity Score by subcategory
